title: verbascum rotundifolium subsp. haenseleri
intro: Verbascum rotundifolium subsp. Haenseleri, also known as Haensel's mullein or round-leaved mullein, is a subspecies of the Verbascum rotundifolium plant, which is a flowering perennial native to the mountainous regions of Europe. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating various health issues such as respiratory problems, wounds, and skin conditions. The medicinal properties of Verbascum rotundifolium subsp. Haenseleri have been attributed to its high mucilage content, which can soothe and protect the mucous membranes, providing relief from coughs, colds, and other respiratory ailments. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its leaves and flowers can be dried and used in potpourri or as a natural dye.
